title: Ansible Custom Facts Collection Change Detection
dataSources: `index=iac` `sourcetype=ansible:callback` with `ansible_facts` subset, or `sourcetype=ansible:result` embedding facts hash
app: Custom facts callback (HEC) emitting host, fact_namespace, facts_sha256
spl:
index=iac sourcetype="ansible:callback" action="facts_snapshot"
| eval fh=coalesce(facts_sha256, facts_hash)
| sort 0 host, fact_namespace, -_time
| streamstats current=f window=2 global=f by host, fact_namespace values(fh) as fh_window
| eval prior=mvindex(fh_window,1)
| eval curr=mvindex(fh_window,0)
| where isnotnull(prior) AND curr!=prior
| table _time, host, fact_namespace, prior, curr, playbook
description: Operator-defined facts (hardware classes, security posture tags, license tiers) often drive playbook branching; unexpected changes can mean tampering, golden-image drift, or failed middleware upgrades. Hashing normalized fact bundles makes diff detection searchable at fleet scale.
value: Speeds root-cause analysis when automation starts skipping hosts or applying wrong templates after fact churn.
implementation: Implement a lightweight callback that serializes selected fact keys, sorts keys, and SHA-256 hashes the JSON. Throttle to daily per host if volume is high. Exclude volatile facts (`date_time`, `ansible_date_time`).
visualization: Changelog table, Sankey (namespace transitions), alert list with drilldown to diff JSON in KV store.
grandmaExplanation: We keep a fingerprint of the custom machine facts your automation relies on. When that fingerprint changes, we log it so you can tell intentional upgrades apart from surprises.
